008 *

017package org.apache.juneau.marshall;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
020
021/**
022 * Supported wire formats for non-finite {@link Float} / {@link Double} values
023 * ({@link Double#NaN NaN}, {@link Double#POSITIVE_INFINITY +Infinity}, {@link Double#NEGATIVE_INFINITY -Infinity}).
024 *
025 * <p>
026 * Used by {@link MarshallingContext.Builder#floatFormat(FloatFormat)},
027 * {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.Marshalled#floatFormat()},
028 * {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshalledProp#floatFormat()}, and
029 * {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshalledConfig#floatFormat()} to control how non-finite
030 * floating-point values are written to text-based wire formats.  Finite values are unaffected and ride
031 * the natural bare-numeric-token wire form for the underlying serializer.
032 *
033 * <p>
034 * The default is {@link #NaN_AS_NULL} which is JSON-spec-compliant per RFC 8259 §6 (non-finite floats
035 * are not valid JSON tokens; emitting {@code null} is the closest valid representation).
036 *
037 * <h5 class='topic'>Precedence (highest to lowest)</h5>
038 * <ol>
039 *    <li>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshalledProp#floatFormat() @MarshalledProp(floatFormat=…)} on the bean property.
040 *    <li>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.Marshalled#floatFormat() @Marshalled(floatFormat=…)} on the bean class.
041 *    <li>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshalledConfig#floatFormat() @MarshalledConfig(floatFormat=…)} on
042 *       <code><ja>@Rest</ja></code>-annotated classes / methods.
043 *    <li>Programmatic {@link MarshallingContext.Builder#floatFormat(FloatFormat)}.
044 *    <li>Environment variable <c>MarshallingContext.floatFormat</c>.
045 *    <li>The default constant ({@link #NaN_AS_NULL}).
046 * </ol>
047 *
048 * <h5 class='topic'>Parser leniency</h5>
049 *
050 * <p>
051 * Parsers SHALL accept all of the following wire shapes regardless of the parser-side
052 * {@code FloatFormat} setting:
053 * <ul>
054 *    <li>Bare numeric tokens including the non-standard {@code NaN} / {@code Infinity} / {@code -Infinity} tokens
055 *       (Juneau's lenient parser accepts these by default).
056 *    <li>Quoted-string forms — {@code "NaN"}, {@code "Infinity"}, {@code "-Infinity"} (case-insensitive).
057 *    <li>{@code null} — reconstructed as {@link Double#NaN} when reading into a primitive {@code double} / {@code float};
058 *       preserved as {@code null} when reading into a boxed {@link Double} / {@link Float}.
059 * </ul>
060 *
061 * <h5 class='topic'>JSON-spec compliance</h5>
062 *
063 * <p>
064 * {@link #NaN_AS_NUMBER} is <b>non-standard JSON</b> per RFC 8259 §6 — JSON does not permit {@code NaN} /
065 * {@code Infinity} / {@code -Infinity} tokens. Juneau's own JSON parser accepts these in lenient mode,
066 * but strict-mode consumers (e.g. some browser {@code JSON.parse} pipelines) will reject them. Use only
067 * when you control both ends of the wire.
068 *
069 * <h5 class='topic'>Binary serializers</h5>
070 *
071 * <p>
072 * Binary serializers (BSON / CBOR / MsgPack / Prototext / Parquet) emit native IEEE-754 floats regardless
073 * of this setting; native non-finite representations exist in every supported binary format (BSON
074 * {@code double} / {@code decimal128}; CBOR's tag 22 / 23 / 24 for special floats; MsgPack {@code float64}).
075 * This setting only affects text-based serializers.
076 */
077@SuppressWarnings({
078   "java:S115" // NaN_AS_* enum constants use the IEEE 754 abbreviation 'NaN' which includes lowercase letters; intentional
079})
080public enum FloatFormat {
081
082   /** Sentinel meaning "no value configured" — falls through to the next-higher precedence level. */
083   NOT_SET,
084
085   /**
086    * Emit {@code null} for {@code NaN} / {@code ±Infinity} (the default).
087    *
088    * <p>
089    * <b>JSON-spec compliant.</b> Loses the ability to distinguish {@code NaN} from
090    * {@code +Infinity} from {@code -Infinity} on the wire — all three become {@code null}. Round-trips
091    * back to {@link Double#NaN} when reading into a primitive {@code double}; into a boxed {@link Double}
092    * field, {@code null} stays {@code null}.
093    */
094   NaN_AS_NULL,
095
096   /**
097    * Emit the quoted string {@code "NaN"} / {@code "Infinity"} / {@code "-Infinity"}.
098    *
099    * <p>
100    * <b>Round-trip preserving.</b> The parser unambiguously decodes the string back to the original
101    * non-finite value.
102    */
103   NaN_AS_STRING,
104
105   /**
106    * Emit the bare {@code NaN} / {@code Infinity} / {@code -Infinity} token.
107    *
108    * <p>
109    * <b>Non-standard JSON</b> per RFC 8259 §6. Accepted by Juneau's lenient parser and V8 / several
110    * Python libs; rejected by strict consumers. Use only when you control both ends of the wire.
111    */
112   NaN_AS_NUMBER,
113
114   /**
115    * Throw {@link IllegalArgumentException} when a non-finite value is encountered.
116    *
117    * <p>
118    * <b>Fail-fast</b> for data-cleanliness pipelines where a {@code NaN} indicates upstream corruption.
119    */
120   NaN_AS_ERROR;
121
122   /**
123    * Formats the specified {@code double} using this format.
124    *
125    * <p>
126    * Finite values always return the boxed {@link Double} so text serializers emit the bare numeric
127    * token. Non-finite values are dispatched per the constant:
128    * <ul>
129    *    <li>{@link #NaN_AS_NULL} → {@code null}.
130    *    <li>{@link #NaN_AS_STRING} → {@code "NaN"} / {@code "Infinity"} / {@code "-Infinity"}.
131    *    <li>{@link #NaN_AS_NUMBER} → boxed {@link Double} (non-finite); text serializers emit the bare token.
132    *    <li>{@link #NaN_AS_ERROR} → throws {@link IllegalArgumentException}.
133    * </ul>
134    *
135    * <p>
136    * {@link #NOT_SET} / {@code null} format are treated as {@link #NaN_AS_NULL}.
137    *
138    * @param value The value to format.
139    * @param format The configured format. Can be <jk>null</jk> (treated as {@link #NaN_AS_NULL}).
140    * @return The formatted value — a {@link Double} for finite or {@code NaN_AS_NUMBER}, a {@link String}
141    *   for {@code NaN_AS_STRING}, or {@code null} for non-finite under {@code NaN_AS_NULL}.
142    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If {@code format} is {@link #NaN_AS_ERROR} and the value is non-finite.
143    */
144   public static Object format(double value, FloatFormat format) {
145      if (Double.isFinite(value))
146         return Double.valueOf(value);
147      return formatNonFinite(value, format);
148   }
149
150   /**
151    * Formats the specified {@code float} using this format.
152    *
153    * <p>
154    * Finite values return the boxed {@link Float}. Non-finite values follow the same rules as
155    * {@link #format(double, FloatFormat)} except that {@link #NaN_AS_NUMBER} returns the boxed
156    * {@link Float} (not {@link Double}).
157    *
158    * @param value The value to format.
159    * @param format The configured format. Can be <jk>null</jk> (treated as {@link #NaN_AS_NULL}).
160    * @return The formatted value — a {@link Float} for finite or {@code NaN_AS_NUMBER}, a {@link String}
161    *   for {@code NaN_AS_STRING}, or {@code null} for non-finite under {@code NaN_AS_NULL}.
162    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If {@code format} is {@link #NaN_AS_ERROR} and the value is non-finite.
163    */
164   public static Object format(float value, FloatFormat format) {
165      if (Float.isFinite(value))
166         return Float.valueOf(value);
167      var fmt = format == null ? NaN_AS_NULL : format;
168      return switch (fmt) {
169         case NOT_SET, NaN_AS_NULL -> null;
170         case NaN_AS_STRING -> nonFiniteTokenFloat(value);
171         case NaN_AS_NUMBER -> Float.valueOf(value);
172         case NaN_AS_ERROR -> throw illegalArg("Non-finite float value {0} rejected by FloatFormat.NaN_AS_ERROR", value);
173      };
174   }
175
176   private static Object formatNonFinite(double value, FloatFormat format) {
177      var fmt = format == null ? NaN_AS_NULL : format;
178      return switch (fmt) {
179         case NOT_SET, NaN_AS_NULL -> null;
180         case NaN_AS_STRING -> nonFiniteToken(value);
181         case NaN_AS_NUMBER -> Double.valueOf(value);
182         case NaN_AS_ERROR -> throw illegalArg("Non-finite double value {0} rejected by FloatFormat.NaN_AS_ERROR", value);
183      };
184   }
185
186   private static String nonFiniteToken(double value) {
187      if (Double.isNaN(value))
188         return "NaN";
189      return value > 0 ? "Infinity" : "-Infinity";
190   }
191
192   private static String nonFiniteTokenFloat(float value) {
193      if (Float.isNaN(value))
194         return "NaN";
195      return value > 0 ? "Infinity" : "-Infinity";
196   }
197
198   /**
199    * Parses the specified wire value into a non-finite-aware {@link Double} or {@link Float}.
200    *
201    * <p>
202    * Lenient parsing — accepts bare numeric, quoted-string {@code "NaN"} / {@code "Infinity"} /
203    * {@code "-Infinity"} (case-insensitive), and unquoted {@code NaN} / {@code Infinity} / {@code -Infinity}
204    * tokens regardless of the {@code format} hint. The hint is informational only.
205    *
206    * @param <T> The target type ({@link Double} or {@link Float}).
207    * @param value The wire value. Can be <jk>null</jk> or blank.
208    * @param format The configured format hint (informational only — parsing is format-agnostic). Can be <jk>null</jk>.
209    * @param targetType The desired return type — {@link Double} or {@link Float}.
210    * @return The parsed value, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code value} is <jk>null</jk> or blank.
211    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If the value cannot be parsed or {@code targetType} is unsupported.
212    */
213   @SuppressWarnings({
214      "unchecked" // Cast is safe: parameterized by caller.
215   })
216   public static <T extends Number> T parse(String value, FloatFormat format, Class<T> targetType) {
217      if (value == null)
218         return null;
219      var s = value.trim();
220      if (s.isEmpty())
221         return null;
222      // Strip surrounding quotes if present (lenient parsing — quoted-string and bare-numeric both accepted).
223      if (s.length() >= 2 && (s.charAt(0) == '"' || s.charAt(0) == '\'') && s.charAt(s.length() - 1) == s.charAt(0))
224         s = s.substring(1, s.length() - 1).trim();
225      try {
226         if (Double.class.equals(targetType) || double.class.equals(targetType))
227            return (T) Double.valueOf(parseDouble(s));
228         if (Float.class.equals(targetType) || float.class.equals(targetType))
229            return (T) Float.valueOf(parseFloat(s));
230         throw illegalArg("Unsupported FloatFormat target type: {0}", targetType.getName());
231      } catch (NumberFormatException e) {
232         throw illegalArg("Invalid float value ''{0}'' for format {1}: {2}", value, format, e.getMessage());
233      }
234   }
235
236   private static double parseDouble(String s) {
237      var lower = s.toLowerCase();
238      return switch (lower) {
239         case "nan" -> Double.NaN;
240         case "infinity", "+infinity", "inf", "+inf" -> Double.POSITIVE_INFINITY;
241         case "-infinity", "-inf" -> Double.NEGATIVE_INFINITY;
242         default -> Double.parseDouble(s);
243      };
244   }
245
246   private static float parseFloat(String s) {
247      var lower = s.toLowerCase();
248      return switch (lower) {
249         case "nan" -> Float.NaN;
250         case "infinity", "+infinity", "inf", "+inf" -> Float.POSITIVE_INFINITY;
251         case "-infinity", "-inf" -> Float.NEGATIVE_INFINITY;
252         default -> Float.parseFloat(s);
253      };
254   }
255
256   /**
257    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this format always emits a bare numeric wire token for non-finite values.
258    *
259    * <p>
260    * Only {@link #NaN_AS_NUMBER} returns <jk>true</jk>. {@link #NaN_AS_NULL} may emit {@code null};
261    * {@link #NaN_AS_STRING} emits a quoted string; {@link #NaN_AS_ERROR} throws. Finite values are
262    * always emitted as bare numeric tokens regardless of this setting.
263    *
264    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this format emits a bare numeric wire token for non-finite values.
265    */
266   public boolean isNumeric() {
267      return this == NaN_AS_NUMBER;
268   }
269}
